objective: Log in to the application as the 'administrator' user without knowing the password.
Make the product-category filter return products that are not released (display all products regardless of release status).
Use a UNION-based SQL injection to retrieve the administrator's username and password from the users table, then log in as administrator.
Exploit an access-control flaw where a user id is taken from a request parameter to obtain the API key of the user 'carlos' and submit it.
Find the unprotected admin panel and use it to delete the user 'carlos'.
Find an insecure direct object reference exposing other users' chat transcripts, and retrieve the password of the user 'carlos'.
Exploit SSRF in the stock-check feature to access the admin interface at http://localhost/admin and delete the user 'carlos'.
Exploit SSRF in the stock-check feature to scan the 192.168.0.0/24 back-end range, find the admin interface on port 8080, and delete the user 'carlos'.

Enumerate a valid username via response differences, brute-force its password (the candidate username/password wordlists are linked on the lab description page), and log in to reach the 'My account' page.
